aids.title: Graph Types: DCWB
aids.content: **D**irected, **C**omplete, **W**eighted, **B**ipartite -- remember 'Don't Confuse With Basics' for the four major graph classifications.
aids.forRemembering: The four main classifications of graphs

aids.title: Handshaking Theorem
aids.content: Every handshake involves two hands. Similarly, every edge touches two vertices. So if there are e handshakes (edges), 2e hands (degree contributions) are involved. Sum of degrees = 2 * edges.
aids.forRemembering: Why sum of all vertex degrees equals twice the number of edges

aids.title: Complete Graph Edge Count
aids.content: For Kn: each of the n vertices connects to (n-1) others, giving n(n-1) directed connections. Divide by 2 for undirected: n(n-1)/2. For K5: 5*4/2 = 10. For K4: 4*3/2 = 6.
aids.forRemembering: Formula for edges in a complete graph

aids.title: In-Degree vs Out-Degree
aids.content: Think of a room: IN-degree = doors people come IN through, OUT-degree = doors people go OUT through. For a digraph vertex, arrows pointing IN = in-degree, arrows pointing OUT = out-degree.
aids.forRemembering: Distinguishing in-degree from out-degree in directed graphs
